content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION message will define the actual memory stage for this model call.
According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY INSTRUCTION text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only instruction.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>
        Pass mode: final_selection

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - This is the final global pass. Prefer precision over keeping marginal context.
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer action items only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact needed for the target answer is selected separately.
        - A single specific style claim is not sufficient for a broad adaptation question when the relevant profile also contains language, tone, formatting, or answer-detail preferences.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or ACTION_ITEM and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: FACTUAL
        Require evidence fallback: false
        Retrieval budget: claims=0 notes=2 actionItems=0 sources=2 episodes=0
        Target message:
        According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<0>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<0>","text":"Entity is the canonical memory node.","context":"02. Data Model / 3.2 Entity","confidence":0.95,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"Канонический узел памяти."}]}
2. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<1>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"Note is not a final claim.","context":"Section 3.4 lists exclusions for Note.","confidence":0.98,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"- не final claim"}]}
3. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<2>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"Note is not a source.","context":"Section 3.4 defines what Note is and is not.","confidence":0.98,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"- не source"}]}
4. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<3>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"Note is used for semantic fragments that are already important and useful for retrieval but should not yet be cemented as hard facts.","context":"Section 3.4 explains when Note should be used.","confidence":0.94,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"`Note` нужен для вещей, которые: - уже важны - полезны для retrieval - но еще не должны быть зацементированы как hard fact"}]}
5. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<4>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"Note should not be left without a maturity model.","context":"Section 5 introduces required maturity handling for Note.","confidence":0.95,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"`Note` нельзя оставлять без модели зрелости."}]}
6. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<5>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<2>","text":"Source is the primary evidence layer that stores chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, and external records.","context":"02. Data Model / 3.1 Source","confidence":0.96,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"- хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records - быть первичным evidence layer"}]}
7. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<6>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<2>","text":"Source supports re-check and explainability.","context":"02. 8. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<7>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<3>","text":"The v3 data model should support temporal truth.","context":"Document section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея'.","confidence":0.98,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"- temporal truth"}]}
9. {"type":"claim","id":"hot-path:claim:<8>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"has_constraint","predicate_family":"has_constraint","subject_entity_id":"entity:<3>","text":"The v3 data model should support typed memory.","context":"Document section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея'.","confidence":0.98,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"IMPORTED","quote":"- typed memory"}]}
10. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","score":0.8413333333333334,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"`Note` is an intermediate semantic fragment for retrieval-ready but non-final knowledge","text":"The `Note` component is positioned between raw source and final claims: it stores important contextual knowledge (e.g., decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) that is useful for retrieval without hardening into immutable fact, with invariants enforcing one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning.","confidence":0.96,"importance":9,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"`Note` нужен для вещей, которые: - уже важны - полезны для retrieval - но еще не должны быть зацементированы как hard fact"}]}
11. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","score":0.8368888888888889,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"v3 memory model layer decomposition and cross-cutting concerns","text":"The v3 data model is intentionally split into layered components—Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate context), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit)—to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantics, lifecycle/retention, and repairability. It also defines two cross-cutting concerns: scope and retrieval lifecycle.","confidence":0.96,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Поэтому модель делится на: - **evidence layer** — `Source` - **graph-shaped semantic layer** — `Entity`, `Claim` - **rich intermediate layer** — `Note` - **projections / operational memory** — `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode` - **audit layer** — `MemoryRun` Плюс у `v3` есть два cross-cutting concern: - ...[truncated 31 chars]"}]}
12. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<2>","score":0.8291666666666666,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Source component role and invariants in v3 data model","text":"In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records for re-check and explainability; key invariants require immutable source content, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, explicit delete policy, and `content_hash`-based dedup support.","confidence":0.95,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"### 3.1 `Source` Сырой источник знания."}]}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | action_item | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | action_item | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector
responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.

structured.fullText: {"rejected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<0>","item_type":"claim","reason":"About Entity, not asked."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<1>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Negative constraint on Note; not needed for brief purpose answer."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<2>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Negative constraint on Note; redundant for concise answer."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<4>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Maturity-model governance detail, not needed for asked definition."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<6>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Extra Source detail (re-check/explainability) beyond minimal required."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<7>","item_type":"claim","reason":"General v3 principle, unrelated to Source/Note purpose question."},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<8>","item_type":"claim","reason":"General v3 principle, unrelated to Source/Note purpose question."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","item_type":"note","reason":"Accurate digest but redundant with selected direct claims."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","item_type":"note","reason":"High-level layer decomposition; broader than needed."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<2>","item_type":"note","reason":"Digest of Source with invariants; extra detail not needed for short answer."}],"selected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<5>","item_type":"claim","rank":1,"reason":"Directly states what Source is for in the data model.","relevance":"direct_answer"},{"item_id":"hot-path:claim:<3>","item_type":"claim","rank":2,"reason":"Directly states what Note is for in the data model.","relevance":"direct_answer"}],"summary":"Selected the two active claims that directly define the purpose of Source and Note, rejecting broader or redundant context."}
